meta.certificationName: Google Cloud Professional Machine Learning Engineer
meta.title: GCP-MLE-4: Feature Engineering, Serving, and ML Operations

questions.stem: A deployment is underperforming because the wrong approach was chosen. If the real requirement is **give practitioners a managed notebook environment for exploration and prototyping**, which correction is BEST?

options.text: Use a batch prediction job
Use only a production endpoint
Use Vertex AI Workbench
options.explanation: Workbench provides managed notebooks integrated with Vertex AI workflows. **A** is incorrect because batch prediction is not an interactive development workspace **B** is incorrect because serving infrastructure is not a notebook environment **D** is incorrect because monitoring is for deployed models, not exploration

options.text: Use Model Monitoring

questions.reference: [Introduction to Agent Platform Workbench  |  Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/workbench/introduction)

questions.stem: A deployment is underperforming because the wrong approach was chosen. If the real requirement is **manage reusable features for both training and low-latency serving**, which correction is BEST?

options.text: Use Vertex AI Feature Store
options.explanation: Feature Store is the managed capability for serving and reusing features across training and online scenarios. **A** is incorrect because prediction jobs consume features rather than acting as a feature catalog **C** is incorrect because pipelines orchestrate workflow steps but are not the feature repository itself **D** is incorrect because logs are not designed to serve online or offline features

options.text: Use Vertex AI Pipelines only
Use batch prediction jobs as the feature repository
Use Cloud Logging only

questions.reference: [About Vertex AI Feature Store  |  Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/featurestore/latest/overview)

questions.stem: A project lead wants to use the managed platform capability that best supports **detect feature drift or prediction skew after a model is deployed**. What should the team choose?

options.text: Use Vertex AI Model Monitoring
options.explanation: Model Monitoring is built to observe deployed models for skew, drift, and related serving-time issues. **A** is incorrect because bigger machines affect capacity, not drift detection **B** is incorrect because generic queries can help analysis but do not replace the managed monitoring feature **D** is incorrect because historic training metrics do not show live drift

options.text: Rely on training accuracy from last month
Increase endpoint machine size
Use only BigQuery scheduled queries with no model telemetry

questions.reference: [Introduction to Model Monitoring  |  Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/model-monitoring/overview)

questions.stem: In this scenario, which option is MOST appropriate for the following requirement: **search model hyperparameters automatically to improve validation performance**?

options.text: Use Vertex AI hyperparameter tuning
options.explanation: Hyperparameter tuning automates exploration of parameter combinations against an objective metric. **A** is incorrect because unguarded random choice is not a reliable optimization method **C** is incorrect because region changes latency, not model search quality **D** is incorrect because dashboards do not optimize hyperparameters

options.text: Change the prediction endpoint region
Skip validation and pick random values
Create more dashboards

questions.reference: [Overview of hyperparameter tuning  |  Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/training/hyperparameter-tuning-overview)

questions.stem: A deployment is underperforming because the wrong approach was chosen. If the real requirement is **search model hyperparameters automatically to improve validation performance**, which correction is BEST?

options.text: Change the prediction endpoint region
Skip validation and pick random values
Use Vertex AI hyperparameter tuning
options.explanation: Hyperparameter tuning automates exploration of parameter combinations against an objective metric. **B** is incorrect because dashboards do not optimize hyperparameters **C** is incorrect because region changes latency, not model search quality **D** is incorrect because unguarded random choice is not a reliable optimization method

options.text: Create more dashboards

questions.reference: [Overview of hyperparameter tuning  |  Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/training/hyperparameter-tuning-overview)

questions.stem: In this scenario, which TWO practices most directly support **review a deployed model for production issues before they become user-visible**? (Select TWO)

options.text: Use endpoint CPU metrics as the only model-quality signal
Track drift or skew signals from live traffic
options.explanation: The correct answers are **B and C** because live-serving signals help reveal when production data no longer matches training expectations **B** is correct because alerts and review loops turn monitoring observations into operational action **C** is incorrect because production data changes over time and can invalidate that assumption **D** is incorrect because resource metrics matter operationally but do not alone measure model behavior

options.text: Define alerting or review workflows around monitoring output
options.explanation: The correct answers are **B and C** because live-serving signals help reveal when production data no longer matches training expectations **B** is correct because alerts and review loops turn monitoring observations into operational action **C** is incorrect because production data changes over time and can invalidate that assumption **D** is incorrect because resource metrics matter operationally but do not alone measure model behavior

options.text: Assume the model is stable forever after successful training

questions.reference: [Introduction to Model Monitoring  |  Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/model-monitoring/overview)

questions.stem: In this scenario, which TWO statements are correct about **review a deployed model for production issues before they become user-visible**? (Select TWO)

options.text: Track drift or skew signals from live traffic
options.explanation: The correct answers are **A and B** because live-serving signals help reveal when production data no longer matches training expectations **D** is correct because alerts and review loops turn monitoring observations into operational action **A** is incorrect because resource metrics matter operationally but do not alone measure model behavior **C** is incorrect because production data changes over time and can invalidate that assumption

options.text: Define alerting or review workflows around monitoring output
options.explanation: The correct answers are **A and B** because live-serving signals help reveal when production data no longer matches training expectations **D** is correct because alerts and review loops turn monitoring observations into operational action **A** is incorrect because resource metrics matter operationally but do not alone measure model behavior **C** is incorrect because production data changes over time and can invalidate that assumption

options.text: Assume the model is stable forever after successful training
Use endpoint CPU metrics as the only model-quality signal

questions.reference: [Introduction to Model Monitoring  |  Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/model-monitoring/overview)

questions.stem: A team is planning a solution and needs to **return predictions synchronously for an interactive application**. Which service or design choice should they make?

options.text: Use hyperparameter tuning instead of serving
Run a nightly batch prediction job
Deploy an online prediction endpoint
options.explanation: Online endpoints are designed for low-latency synchronous inference requests. **A** is incorrect because weekly exports are stale for interactive use **B** is incorrect because tuning optimizes model search rather than exposes inference **C** is incorrect because batch jobs are for offline scoring, not request-time responses

options.text: Export predictions to Cloud Storage once per week

questions.reference: [Get online inferences from a custom trained model  |  Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/predictions/get-online-predictions)

questions.stem: A team is planning a solution and needs to **choose a single managed platform for building, training, serving, and governing ML workloads on Google Cloud**. Which service or design choice should they make?

options.text: Use Looker dashboards as the primary ML platform
Use Cloud Storage as the only ML platform component
Use Vertex AI as the unified ML platform
options.explanation: Vertex AI is positioned as Google Cloud's managed platform spanning major ML lifecycle tasks. **B** is incorrect because object storage is only one supporting building block **C** is incorrect because fragmentation increases operational burden when a managed platform is available **D** is incorrect because BI tooling is not the central ML lifecycle platform

options.text: Build separate custom services for every lifecycle stage by default

questions.reference: [Introduction to machine learning on Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/start/introduction-unified-platform)

questions.stem: An architecture review found weak decisions around **review a deployed model for production issues before they become user-visible**. Which TWO changes would best align the design? (Select TWO)

options.text: Define alerting or review workflows around monitoring output
options.explanation: The correct answers are **A and D** because live-serving signals help reveal when production data no longer matches training expectations **B** is correct because alerts and review loops turn monitoring observations into operational action **C** is incorrect because production data changes over time and can invalidate that assumption **D** is incorrect because resource metrics matter operationally but do not alone measure model behavior

options.text: Use endpoint CPU metrics as the only model-quality signal
Assume the model is stable forever after successful training
Track drift or skew signals from live traffic
options.explanation: The correct answers are **A and D** because live-serving signals help reveal when production data no longer matches training expectations **B** is correct because alerts and review loops turn monitoring observations into operational action **C** is incorrect because production data changes over time and can invalidate that assumption **D** is incorrect because resource metrics matter operationally but do not alone measure model behavior

questions.reference: [Introduction to Model Monitoring  |  Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/model-monitoring/overview)

questions.stem: A project lead wants to use the managed platform capability that best supports **search model hyperparameters automatically to improve validation performance**. What should the team choose?

options.text: Change the prediction endpoint region
Create more dashboards
Use Vertex AI hyperparameter tuning
options.explanation: Hyperparameter tuning automates exploration of parameter combinations against an objective metric. **A** is incorrect because unguarded random choice is not a reliable optimization method **B** is incorrect because dashboards do not optimize hyperparameters **D** is incorrect because region changes latency, not model search quality

options.text: Skip validation and pick random values

questions.reference: [Overview of hyperparameter tuning  |  Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/training/hyperparameter-tuning-overview)

questions.stem: A team is planning a solution and needs to **score a very large dataset offline without a human waiting for each result**. Which service or design choice should they make?

options.text: Use an online endpoint for every row interactively
Run a batch prediction job
options.explanation: Batch prediction is intended for large asynchronous scoring workloads against datasets. **A** is incorrect because online inference is inefficient for bulk offline scoring **C** is incorrect because notebooks are not the managed large-scale scoring path **D** is incorrect because monitoring observes models but does not perform scoring

options.text: Use model monitoring only
Deploy a single-user notebook

questions.reference: [Get batch inferences from a custom trained model  |  Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/predictions/get-batch-predictions)

questions.stem: A deployment is underperforming because the wrong approach was chosen. If the real requirement is **orchestrate repeatable ML steps with lineage and reusable components**, which correction is BEST?

options.text: Use a single online endpoint for every step
Use Vertex AI Pipelines
options.explanation: Pipelines provide orchestration, lineage, and reusable components for repeatable ML workflows. **A** is incorrect because manual notebook execution is harder to reproduce and operate **B** is incorrect because monitoring detects issues but is not the pipeline engine **D** is incorrect because online endpoints serve inference rather than orchestrate workflows

options.text: Use only ad hoc notebooks
Use model monitoring alerts as the orchestrator

questions.reference: [Introduction to Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform Pipelines](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/pipelines/introduction)

options.text: Use Model Monitoring

options.explanation: Workbench provides managed notebooks integrated with Vertex AI workflows. **A** is incorrect because serving infrastructure is not a notebook environment **B** is incorrect because batch prediction is not an interactive development workspace **C** is incorrect because monitoring is for deployed models, not exploration

questions.stem: In this scenario, which TWO practices most directly support **move from a one-off notebook experiment to a production-ready repeatable training pipeline**? (Select TWO)

options.text: Package the steps as pipeline components with defined inputs and outputs
options.explanation: The correct answers are **A and D** because componentized steps are easier to automate and reuse **D** is correct because lineage and execution records are core parts of operational ML workflows **B** is incorrect because local-only workflow definitions are not production-ready **C** is incorrect because manual execution is brittle and hard to audit

options.text: Store the only copy of the workflow in a developer's local session
Rerun notebook cells manually whenever production retraining is needed
Record artifacts and execution history through the managed orchestration system
options.explanation: The correct answers are **A and D** because componentized steps are easier to automate and reuse **D** is correct because lineage and execution records are core parts of operational ML workflows **B** is incorrect because local-only workflow definitions are not production-ready **C** is incorrect because manual execution is brittle and hard to audit

questions.reference: [Introduction to Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform Pipelines](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/pipelines/introduction)

options.text: Use Vertex AI Pipelines
options.explanation: Pipelines provide orchestration, lineage, and reusable components for repeatable ML workflows. **A** is incorrect because online endpoints serve inference rather than orchestrate workflows **C** is incorrect because monitoring detects issues but is not the pipeline engine **D** is incorrect because manual notebook execution is harder to reproduce and operate

options.text: Use only ad hoc notebooks
Use model monitoring alerts as the orchestrator
Use a single online endpoint for every step

questions.reference: [Introduction to Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform Pipelines](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/pipelines/introduction)

questions.stem: In this scenario, which TWO statements are correct about **move from a one-off notebook experiment to a production-ready repeatable training pipeline**? (Select TWO)

options.text: Record artifacts and execution history through the managed orchestration system
options.explanation: The correct answers are **A and B** because componentized steps are easier to automate and reuse **D** is correct because lineage and execution records are core parts of operational ML workflows **B** is incorrect because manual execution is brittle and hard to audit **C** is incorrect because local-only workflow definitions are not production-ready

options.text: Package the steps as pipeline components with defined inputs and outputs
options.explanation: The correct answers are **A and B** because componentized steps are easier to automate and reuse **D** is correct because lineage and execution records are core parts of operational ML workflows **B** is incorrect because manual execution is brittle and hard to audit **C** is incorrect because local-only workflow definitions are not production-ready

options.text: Store the only copy of the workflow in a developer's local session
Rerun notebook cells manually whenever production retraining is needed

questions.reference: [Introduction to Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform Pipelines](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/pipelines/introduction)

questions.stem: In this scenario, which option is MOST appropriate for the following requirement: **detect feature drift or prediction skew after a model is deployed**?

options.text: Increase endpoint machine size
Use Vertex AI Model Monitoring
options.explanation: Model Monitoring is built to observe deployed models for skew, drift, and related serving-time issues. **B** is incorrect because historic training metrics do not show live drift **C** is incorrect because generic queries can help analysis but do not replace the managed monitoring feature **D** is incorrect because bigger machines affect capacity, not drift detection

options.text: Use only BigQuery scheduled queries with no model telemetry
Rely on training accuracy from last month

questions.reference: [Introduction to Model Monitoring  |  Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/model-monitoring/overview)
